featureName: BUG-099-002: the test-lane orchestrators (smackerel.sh integration / integration-light / e2e) and runtime-health scripts call the GNU `timeout` binary (and its GNU-only --kill-after flag) directly with no gtimeout/watchdog fallback, so on macOS (no bare `timeout` on PATH) the lane fails with `timeout: command not found` (exit 127) AFTER the pre-flight passes — blocking the stores-up + Go-test run on every macOS host (wsl-macos-compatibility violation)
discoveredBy: bubbles.devops — during the BUG-099-001 integration-light re-run on a macOS host (surfaced AFTER the pre-flight passed)
rootCause: Test-lane orchestrators + runtime-health scripts invoke the GNU `timeout` binary (with GNU-only `timeout --kill-after`) directly. macOS ships no bare `timeout` on PATH (GNU coreutils installs it as `gtimeout`), and the scripts have NO timeout->gtimeout->watchdog fallback. scripts/lib/runtime.sh has no portable timeout helper. Result on macOS: `timeout: command not found` (exit 127) at the first bare-timeout call, blocking the stores-up + Go-test run AFTER BUG-099-001's pre-flight fix lets the lane proceed. NOTE: docker-compose's own `down --timeout` / `up --wait --wait-timeout` flags are unaffected — only the bare `timeout` binary call sites are broken.
recommendedFix: Add a portable resolver smackerel_run_with_timeout (timeout -> gtimeout -> watchdog, exit-124 preserved) to scripts/lib/runtime.sh per .github/instructions/wsl-macos-compatibility.instructions.md, then replace every bare `timeout [--kill-after=Xs] N CMD` call site with it. Validate shellcheck -x + bash -n; re-run test integration-light (to land BUG-069-002/003 order-2 durability evidence) + test integration + test e2e (to prove heavy + e2e lanes complete on macOS).
evidence: Real re-run capture (macOS, 2026-06-30): pre-flight 'OK / RAM available: 8024 MB (required >= 2000 MB)' THEN './smackerel.sh: line 1207: timeout: command not found' + teardown 'line 1186: timeout: command not found' + 'integration-light stack teardown failed during exit cleanup (exit 127)' + '=== integration-light exit=127 ==='. `command -v timeout` = ABSENT; `command -v gtimeout` = present. `docker ps --filter name=smackerel` = empty (no stack started; nothing leaked).
blockedOps: ./smackerel.sh --env test test integration-light (stores-up + durability Go test) on macOS
./smackerel.sh test integration (heavy) — same bare-timeout teardown/health-gate calls (lines 1063, 1084)
affectedSurface: scripts/lib/runtime.sh (no portable timeout helper exists today — fix lands here)

fixSequence.task: Add smackerel_run_with_timeout (timeout -> gtimeout -> watchdog, exit-124 preserved) to scripts/lib/runtime.sh; replace every bare `timeout [--kill-after=Xs] N CMD` call site in smackerel.sh (integration / integration-light / e2e lanes) and the two runtime-health scripts. Validate shellcheck -x + bash -n. Re-run test integration-light (land BUG-069-002/003 order-2 durability evidence), test integration, and test e2e to prove all three lanes complete on macOS. NOT done in the BUG-099-001 task (cross-lane change beyond that task's targeted scope).
